Hey Alex, thanks for the detailed answer and sharing your perspective. It's an POV and I completely agree.

Hey Hari, I always look for evidence of driving business value. Most data scientists can build a churn model, look at feature importance, or find a customer's propensity to do something (upgrade, activate, download, etc), but making that model useful to the business is hard. A model that doesn't actually address a business need isn't very useful to anyone and arguably just introduces tech debt. Many entry level data scientists come from an analytics background  so I encourage those folks to also highlight times where their insights helped the business.   I work specifically within data science insights (not ML) so it's important that our data scientists are always asking 'why' they are doing something. If a data scientist understands the root of a problem, they can recommend much better solutions.

Hi Pooja,  This is Hari, a recent grad from Columbia University.  Being an international student myself, I applied to over 500 jobs before landing my first role. I struggled to maintain all this information in spreadsheets through manual entry. I wanted to automate this and make job tracking easier which is why I created JotterWolf (a free tool).  A little more information about JotterWolf - It is a Job Tracker empowered with ChatGPT. By installing the chrome extension jobseekers are just one step away from adding all job role details in a centralized tracker with job openings, applications, notes and more! The ChatGPT extension allows users to pair the job description and their resume and helps them prepare for all that is to come in the interview for that specific role. We also have other features such as Weekly Goal Setter and Job Tracking Analytics inbuilt in the tracker. With this tool, we want to help job-seekers in being prepared for that crucial interview call!  Link - https://jotterwolf.com/  If you think this tool would be helpful for everyone, could you spread the word about Jotterwolf to our international students LinkedIn community?  Looking forward to getting your feedback on this tool.  With kind regards, Hari

Hi Yudi J,  This is Hari, a recent grad from Columbia University.  Being an international student myself, I applied to over 500 jobs before landing my first role. I struggled to maintain all this information in spreadsheets through manual entry. I wanted to automate this and make job tracking easier which is why I created JotterWolf (a free tool).  A little more information about JotterWolf - It is a Job Tracker empowered with ChatGPT. By installing the chrome extension jobseekers are just one step away from adding all job role details in a centralized tracker with job openings, applications, notes and more! The ChatGPT extension allows users to pair the  job description and their resume and helps them prepare for all that is to come in the interview for that specific role. We also have other features such as Weekly Goal Setter and Job Tracking Analytics inbuilt in the tracker. With this tool, we want to help job-seekers in being prepared for that crucial interview call!  Link - https://jotterwolf.com/  If you think this tool would be helpful for everyone, could you spread the word about Jotterwolf to our international students LinkedIn community? Hi Robert,  This is Hari, a recent grad from Columbia University.  Being an international student myself, I applied to over 500 jobs before landing my first role. I struggled to maintain all this information in spreadsheets through manual entry. I wanted to automate this and make job tracking easier which is why I created JotterWolf (a free tool).  A little more information about JotterWolf - It is a Job Tracker empowered with ChatGPT. By installing the chrome extension jobseekers are just one step away from adding all job role details in a centralized tracker with job openings, applications, notes and more! The ChatGPT extension allows users to pair the job description and their resume and helps them prepare for all that is to come in the interview for that specific role. We also have other features such as Weekly Goal Setter and Job Tracking Analytics inbuilt in the tracker. With this tool, we want to help job-seekers in being prepared for that crucial interview call!  Link - https://jotterwolf.com/  If you think this tool would be helpful for everyone, could you spread the word about Jotterwolf to our international students LinkedIn community? Hi Hari,  1. How does GWU support my entrepreneurial dream? This probably depends on how you shape your degree and which courses you end up taking throughout the semesters. The DS program at its core offers courses in Mining, ML, NLP, and Deep Learning.  2. How about job opportunities after graduation? Based on the amount of experience you have there are good opportunities out there. Almost all my seniors have secured jobs in big companies including Amazon. More work experience is always good. But if you know your field you can definitely get jobs. 3. Does there any dedicated Research group for Data Science? And particularly for NLP research? The program is fairly new but the department works to make challenges available. There is always a probability that some professors are working on things you are interested in. So approaching them will be a good idea. You have the option to take up Data Science Research as a subject where you will work on research of your choice with the guidance of a faculty. This can include any domains including NLP. 4. Does Coop or Internship option available? Co-op or internships are the student's choice. The university does not give any credits if you pursue either.  5. How about TA, RA and other part time jobs? There are all available in general. Due to COVID, on-campus are scarce, but you can always become a TA or RA after your first semester here.  6. What is the total tution fee (approx)? Are there any scholarships available? Scholarships are available. You need to specify that in your application. Tuition fees will depend on year to year. In my batch, it was close to $50,000. 7. And your personal opinion about the university? Pros and cons? I have a positive outlook towards the university, DC, and the course in general. We need to put in the effort. Sure, I will answer these. 1. There is an Innovation and Entrepreneurship center at WPI but I've mostly seen undergrads be a part of it. You can search more about it online and I can give you the contact of an undergrad who is involved with it. 2. The job opportunities mostly are a result of your own proactive job search. There are career fairs at WPI, but for data science, it rarely is fruitful. A noteworthy thing is WPI does help with your resume editing. In the US, I believe most people get a job as a result of effective networking. WPI is considered a good college in Massachusetts but it may not be the case outside. COVID has definitely impacted the job scene. So keep that in mind. 3. Mostly professors have their research groups. So look for professors' research interests on the WPI site. This might be a good place to start. https://www.wpi.edu/academics/departments/computer-science/research 4. Yes, co-op and internship opportunities are available but you apply on your own and it's your own skillset that gets you the job. As I said, there are career fairs at WPI but it's not like you are guaranteed one. Although, there is a good aspect of MS in Data science here, which is a Graduate qualifying project for 3 credits. You get to work with a company handling their real world data, so that is a good thing to put on your resume. Alternatively, you could pursue a thesis route for 9 credits. 5. Yes, part time jobs are available. Most people do dining jobs or grader part-time jobs. 6. For Data science in my year, there are 33 credits, and each credit is for ~1600$. I don't know if there is any change to these numbers in the new academic year. I haven't heard of any scholarships for data science at the Masters level at WPI. PhD is funded. Most Masters students earn through these part-time jobs during the semester. 7. Personally, I like it. A pro is that there has been a decent mix of courses and electives were pretty flexible too. However, it depends on your profile if WPI is a catch for you. A con is that WPI isn't that well known outside Massachusetts. As with any university in the US, unless we are talking Ivy league, the purpose is to give you merely a platform, to learn and hone your abilities. Ultimately, the job search is going to be a task you personally invest a lot of time in to succeed and I believe university doesn't play that big of a role in that.

If you do your job correctly, and work in getting a good job, the opportunities are there. - I took the statistics track, and they have done a really good job getting professors from universities such as Harvard, Yale and Columbia.  - You can do the master in 3 semesters (I did that) or you can do it 4 (expanding your visa) - Being close to NY is a really big plus, not only for job opportunities, but also if you enjoy going there for other stuff. - I see that you have Computer Science skills, so I think that the statistics track should be a good complement. Cons: - New Brunswick is not a very nice city, and there's not so much to see there. Having a car is a big change once you get one. It's not really necessary, but it's a big change - You're in a university that is ranked around 70, so some big firms sometimes don't even look at your resume. - Don't expect to learn lots of CS skills in the statistics track, is very statistically focus. CS courses are not good  Bottom line: if you can go to a better ranked University, is probably best, but if go here is a good option too. It's well located (NY and it's cheaper to live) and curriculum is good except the CS ones. Some semesters have a lot of work, but if you organize yourself, it can be done and also you can have time to work on your business  I hope this answered most of your questions. Dear Sir/Madam,

Financial Planning is at the core of every successful company, successful family, and successful individual.

Governments have yearly and five-yearly budgets. Corporates have yearly budgets and 3 – 5 year projections. However, as individuals, we must budget for the following timeframes:

1) monthly and annual

2) short-term (1 – 4 years) and long-term (5 – 14 years) 

3) Very long-term (15+ years) or Ultra long-term (30+ years). 

Planning for the long-term and ultra long term is a challenge. The planning and starting the protection and investment at a young age cultivates financial discipline. To understand your goals and guide you, I follow the process and procedures I have outlined below.

The Process:

As a Financial Planner, I look at every client’s needs and obtain their financial goals. After which, we will discuss in-depth about the client’s current Income, Expenses, Assets, and Liabilities, allowing me to arrive at the appropriate solutions. I will also analyze the client’s current status of Insurance and Investments to understand how we can further bolster protection.

The Procedure:

All Financial Planning with me will be done in a professional way, with a long-term, holistic approach. I will go into further detail in our upcoming discussions.

Need for Financial Planning:

Why Financial Planning and which factors should be considered?

On the one hand, there are various market and external forces that are beyond our control, but on the other hand there are numerous investment avenues that we can employ to prepare ourselves for these factors. 

In simple terms, "Plan for the Ds".

In Financial Planning it is important to prepare for unknown "Ds", i.e., Disease, Disability, Damage, Disaster, and Death.

Disease: This can be covered by a Health Insurance Planning. I am a Health Planner with Religare Health Insurance, a standalone Health Insurance, offering professional service.

Damage: Damage to your property purchased through hard earned money, can cause financial burden. There is compulsory, Third Party Liability Insurance for Two Wheeler/Car/other commercial vehicle. It is also advisable to take the Own damage cover to protect the property. Other property cover like Home Insurance is important.

Disability: Personal Accident cover and Overseas Travel covers are essential.

Disaster: This could be either natural or man-made. We need to protect our property from Floods, Earthquakes, Tsunamis, etc. Also there could be "economic" disasters, such as, Dot.com, Global Financial meltdown, or pandemic. All of these unfortunate disasters need to be appropriately planned for.

Death: God Forbid, if the family’s bread-winner meets with an unfortunate premature death, then the family should be able to continue the same standard of life with an adequate Life Insurance cover.

Moving from the topic of Protection to Investment, the value of money today is different from the value of money tomorrow. Inflation is a key factor to account for. To beat inflation, you may have to invest in Mutual Funds. A professional fund house will manage your funds efficiently.


"Plan M" – Plan your Money by Planting your Money

In short, money should be invested appropriately based on your risk appetite. A lot can be said or written about it, however timing the investment and choosing the right investment devices is crucial. There is certainty that you should start early whether it be Insurance (to save cost on premiums) or Investments for.

Kindly give us a chance so that you can understand our various offerings.


Looking forward to discussing further with you and supporting you in “Planting your Money”.


Best Regards,
